reasoning: I can't share my detailed chain-of-thought, but here is a concise summary of the article.
output: Oxford's Campaign to Protect Rural England (CPRE) Oxfordshire plans a High Court judicial review of the Castle Mill development at Port Meadow, arguing the council failed to conduct an environmental impact assessment. The five-storey blocks provide 439 flats and overlook a Site of Special Scientific Interest and a Scheduled Ancient Monument, with CPRE claiming the height and light pollution will spoil Oxford's historic skyline; they also contest the adequacy of proposed mitigation. The council says the challenge is late since construction has completed, and CPRE's claims are unfounded. The University of Oxford notes ongoing discussions on mitigation and plans to occupy the buildings by October, while an independent council review is underway and a petition has attracted over 3,000 signatures.
